parsed.artifact_title: Non-Inferiority Preconditions for the Append-Only Baseline Comparison

claims.text: The protocol gates scheduler viability separately from artifact quality.
Artifact non-inferiority uses a preregistered 0.10 margin against append-only mean quality.
Observability is scored separately and cannot rescue an inferior artifact-quality result.

parsed.conclusion: Before a claim of non-inferiority to the append-only baseline can be made, three conditions must simultaneously hold: (1) the scheduler itself must be separately viable, as gated by the protocol; (2) artifact quality must fall within the preregistered 0.10 margin against append-only mean quality; and (3) observability cannot compensate for an artifact-quality result that is inferior — it is scored separately and cannot rescue the comparison.
declared_losses: This note enumerates what the protocol requires but does not verify whether those gates are actually implemented in the current experiment run — it only states the preconditions that must be satisfied before a non-inferiority claim.
parsed.recommended_action: When evaluating a comparison against the append-only baseline, confirm each of the three preconditions in sequence: pass the scheduler viability gate, check the 0.10 artifact-quality margin, and verify that observability is not being used to offset an inferior artifact result. Only after all three hold may non-inferiority be reported.

content.text: The protocol gates scheduler viability separately from artifact quality.
Artifact non-inferiority uses a preregistered 0.10 margin against append-only mean quality.
Observability is scored separately and cannot rescue an inferior artifact-quality result.

request_payload.instruction: Write the final artifact. Cite only evidence records that support claims. Do not cite distractors. If a limitation remains, include it in declared_losses.
request_payload.task: Write a note deciding what must be true before claiming non-inferiority to the append-only baseline.
